revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん）は、群馬...'
revision.text:
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]竜舞字塚廻りをはじめ5つの小字に分布する古墳群である<ref>[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編 『日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-378頁</ref>。所在する[[古墳]]のうち、4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県の史跡]]に指定され、古墳公園として復元整備されている<ref>[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html 塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳 太田市]</ref>。また古墳群から出土した埴輪は「上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪」の名称で一括して重要文化財に指定されている<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
[[渡良瀬川]]によって形成された標高30m前後の広大な沖積平野中に位置する。[[1997年]]県営圃場整備事業に際し、水田下より発見された。東西300m、南北400mの範囲に[[帆立貝形古墳]]4基(1・2・3・4号墳)、円墳3基(5・6・7号墳)が分布する。
元来、この地は小河川が乱流する起伏に富んだ地形を呈しており、本古墳群も南に延びる馬の背状の低台地先端部に立地する。これが渡良瀬川の沖積作用により一面平坦化され、古墳群が埋没したのは[[奈良時代]]頃と思われる。
== 古墳 == [[ファイル:Ota Gunma Tsukamawari No4 Tumulus 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|4号墳]] '''1号墳'''は全長26.1m、後円部径18.8m、前方部幅10.8mを測る帆立貝形古墳。墳丘と相似形の周堀を有し、周堀を含めた主軸全長は34.1mを測る。墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]がめぐり、前方部の円筒埴輪列の内側を中心に[[形象埴輪]]群も配置されていた。形象埴輪には人物6・大刀4・靫2がある。内部施設は後円部に位置し、墳丘の主軸と直交して置かれた[[箱形石棺]]と推定される。石棺部は削平され堀方と石棺床面に敷かれた粘土層を残すのみである。副葬品はわずかに金環と水晶製算盤玉だけが検出され、その他の遺物も[[土師器]]坏身1、[[須恵器]][[甕]]1のみである。
'''2号墳'''は全長24m、後円部径19m、前方部幅8mを測る。周堀を有し、それを含めた主軸全長は28mとなる。埴輪は円筒埴輪のほかに盾1がある。
'''3号墳'''は全長23.6m、後円部径16.7m、前方部幅7.1mを測り、墳丘の相似形の周堀がめぐる。周堀を含めた主軸全長は31.4m。埴輪には円筒埴輪と形象埴輪(人物10・盾3・大刀4)がある。
'''4号墳'''は調査古墳の中で最も良く原状をとどめ、全長26.1m、後円部径18.8m、前方部幅10.8mを測る帆立貝形古墳で、墳丘と相似形の周堀を有する。周堀を含めた主軸全長は31.4m。墳丘には埴輪列がめぐり、円筒埴輪269(普通253・朝顔16)、形象埴輪35(人物14・家1・盾3・大刀5・馬2など)が検出された。内部施設は後円部に築かれた2基の箱式石棺である。1号石棺は後円部中央よりやや南東寄りに位置し、堀方と石棺床面に敷かれた粘土層が検出された。石棺の規模は長辺2.2m、短辺0.5mと推定される。2号石棺は後円部北側の円筒埴輪列を壊して構築している。長辺2.7m、短辺1.3mの堀方の中に割石を用いて箱式石棺を築き、その上を厚さ5～10cm粘土で被覆している。1・2号石棺からは共に副葬品は検出されなかった。埴輪以外の出土遺物は土師器坏3のみである。
本古墳群の築造過程は、埴輪以外の出土遺物が極めて少ないため詳細は明らかで無いが、1号墳の須恵器、4号墳の土師器は6世紀初頭から中葉の様相を示しており、短期間に群形成がなされたと思われる。また、墳丘は帆立貝形を呈するものの規模の点では、同時代の[[前方後円墳]]とはかなりの隔たりがある。このようなことを勘案すれば、本古墳群は初期群集墳の一形態と理解される。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tomo/tukamawari.html 塚廻り古墳群 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団]

'''4号墳'''は調査古墳の中で最も良く原状をとどめ、全長26.1m、後円部径18.8m、前方部幅10.8mを測る帆立貝形古墳で、墳丘と相似形の周堀を有する。周堀を含めた主軸全長は31.4m。墳丘には埴輪列がめぐり、円筒埴輪269(普通253・朝顔16)、形象埴輪35(人物14・家1・盾3・大刀5・馬2など)が検出された。内部施設は後円部に築かれた2基の箱式石棺である。1号石棺は後円部中央よりやや南東寄りに位置し、堀方と石棺床面に敷かれた粘土層が検出された。石棺の規模は長辺2.2m、短辺0.5mと推定される。2号石棺は後円部北側の円筒埴輪列を壊して構築している。長辺2.7m、短辺1.3mの堀方の中に割石を用いて箱式石棺を築き、その上を厚さ5～10cm粘土で被覆している。1・2号石棺からは共に副葬品は検出されなかった。埴輪以外の出土遺物は土師器坏3のみである。
本古墳群の築造過程は、埴輪以外の出土遺物が極めて少ないため詳細は明らかで無いが、1号墳の須恵器、4号墳の土師器は6世紀初頭から中葉の様相を示しており、短期間に群形成がなされたと思われる。また、墳丘は帆立貝形を呈するものの規模の点では、同時代の[[前方後円墳]]とはかなりの隔たりがある。このようなことを勘案すれば、本古墳群は初期群集墳の一形態と理解される。

{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:つかまわりこふんくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:太田市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県の重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]竜舞字塚廻りをはじめ5つの小字に分布する古墳群である<ref>[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-378頁。</ref>。所在する[[古墳]]のうち、4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県の史跡]]に指定され、古墳公園として復元整備されている<ref>[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html 塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳 太田市]</ref>。また古墳群から出土した埴輪は「上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪」の名称で一括して重要文化財に指定されている<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
[[渡良瀬川]]によって形成された標高30m前後の広大な沖積平野中に位置する。[[1997年]]県営圃場整備事業に際し、水田下より発見された。東西300m、南北400mの範囲に[[帆立貝形古墳]]4基(1・2・3・4号墳)、円墳3基(5・6・7号墳)が分布する。
元来、この地は小河川が乱流する起伏に富んだ地形を呈しており、本古墳群も南に延びる馬の背状の低台地先端部に立地する。これが渡良瀬川の沖積作用により一面平坦化され、古墳群が埋没したのは[[奈良時代]]頃と思われる。
== 古墳 == [[ファイル:Ota Gunma Tsukamawari No4 Tumulus 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|4号墳]] '''1号墳'''は全長26.1m、後円部径18.8m、前方部幅10.8mを測る帆立貝形古墳。墳丘と相似形の周堀を有し、周堀を含めた主軸全長は34.1mを測る。墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]がめぐり、前方部の円筒埴輪列の内側を中心に[[形象埴輪]]群も配置されていた。形象埴輪には人物6・大刀4・靫2がある。内部施設は後円部に位置し、墳丘の主軸と直交して置かれた[[箱形石棺]]と推定される。石棺部は削平され堀方と石棺床面に敷かれた粘土層を残すのみである。副葬品はわずかに金環と水晶製算盤玉だけが検出され、その他の遺物も[[土師器]]坏身1、[[須恵器]][[甕]]1のみである。
'''2号墳'''は全長24m、後円部径19m、前方部幅8mを測る。周堀を有し、それを含めた主軸全長は28mとなる。埴輪は円筒埴輪のほかに盾1がある。
'''3号墳'''は全長23.6m、後円部径16.7m、前方部幅7.1mを測り、墳丘の相似形の周堀がめぐる。周堀を含めた主軸全長は31.4m。埴輪には円筒埴輪と形象埴輪(人物10・盾3・大刀4)がある。
'''4号墳'''は調査古墳の中で最も良く原状をとどめ、全長26.1m、後円部径18.8m、前方部幅10.8mを測る帆立貝形古墳で、墳丘と相似形の周堀を有する。周堀を含めた主軸全長は31.4m。墳丘には埴輪列がめぐり、円筒埴輪269(普通253・朝顔16)、形象埴輪35(人物14・家1・盾3・大刀5・馬2など)が検出された。内部施設は後円部に築かれた2基の箱式石棺である。1号石棺は後円部中央よりやや南東寄りに位置し、堀方と石棺床面に敷かれた粘土層が検出された。石棺の規模は長辺2.2m、短辺0.5mと推定される。2号石棺は後円部北側の円筒埴輪列を壊して構築している。長辺2.7m、短辺1.3mの堀方の中に割石を用いて箱式石棺を築き、その上を厚さ5～10cm粘土で被覆している。1・2号石棺からは共に副葬品は検出されなかった。埴輪以外の出土遺物は土師器坏3のみである。
本古墳群の築造過程は、埴輪以外の出土遺物が極めて少ないため詳細は明らかで無いが、1号墳の須恵器、4号墳の土師器は6世紀初頭から中葉の様相を示しており、短期間に群形成がなされたと思われる。また、墳丘は帆立貝形を呈するものの規模の点では、同時代の[[前方後円墳]]とはかなりの隔たりがある。このようなことを勘案すれば、本古墳群は初期群集墳の一形態と理解される。

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]に存在する古墳群である<ref>[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-374頁。</ref>。所在する[[古墳]]のうち、3号墳、4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県指定史跡]]であり、古墳公園として復元整備されている<ref>[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html 塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳 太田市]</ref>。また古墳群から出土した埴輪は「上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪」の名称で一括して[[重要文化財]]に指定されている<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。

{{混同|塚廻古墳}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[ファイル:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|塚廻り4号墳 埴輪群（復元）}}]] '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん、塚廻古墳群）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]龍舞町にある[[古墳群]]<ref>[[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-374頁。</ref>。4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県指定史跡]]に指定され、古墳群出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。
現在では、4号墳は3号墳前方部とともに古墳公園として復元整備されている<ref>[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html 塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳 太田市]</ref>。

== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は国（文化庁）、群馬県立歴史博物館保管。1985年（昭和60年）6月6日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10022|上野塚廻り古墳群出土埴輪}}</ref> ** 第1号墳出土 *** 盾持男子像 1箇 ** 第3号墳出土 *** 女子倚像 1箇 *** 盾 2箇 ** 第4号墳出土 *** 女子像 2箇 *** 馬 1箇 *** 大刀（基台共） 2箇 ** 附指定 *** 人物残欠 26箇分 *** 馬残欠 3箇分 *** 大刀残欠 9箇分 *** 靱残欠 2箇分 *** 盾残欠 5箇分 *** 家残欠 1箇分 *** 基台 残欠共 一括 *** 円筒埴輪 残欠共 一括
=== 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 塚廻古墳群第4号古墳 - 1977年（昭和52年）9月20日指定<ref name="群馬県">{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。</ref>。

{{混同|塚廻古墳}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[ファイル:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|塚廻り4号墳 埴輪群（復元）}}]] '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん、塚廻古墳群）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]龍舞町にある[[古墳群]][[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-374頁。。4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県指定史跡]]に指定され、古墳群出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 現在では、4号墳は3号墳前方部とともに古墳公園として復元整備されている[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html　塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳　太田市]。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+塚廻り古墳群の一覧 |- ! 古墳名 ||規模 || 形状 || 備考 |- | 1号墳4号墳北北西約300mの位置に所在 || 26.1m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 削平 |- | 2号墳4号墳北西約300mの位置に所在|| 24m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 削平 |- | 3号墳4号墳北に所在|| {{Sdash}} || 帆立貝型古墳 || 一部保存 |- | 4号墳|| 22.5m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 復元、県史跡 |- | 5号墳4号墳南南西約90mの位置に所在|| 16m || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 6号墳4号墳北西約290mの位置に所在、2号墳のすぐ南|| 19m || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 7号墳4号墳北北西約200mの位置に所在|| 20m || 円墳 || 削平 |- | 8号墳 || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 9号墳 || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 10号墳4号墳南の近接地の地表下に所在|| {{Sdash}} || 帆立貝型古墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 11号墳4号墳南西の近接地の地表下に所在 || 16.1m || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 12号墳4号墳西の近接地の地表下に所在 || 9.5m || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 13号墳4号墳北西の近接地の地表下に所在 || {{Sdash}} || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |} == 画像 == 注記以外は群馬県立歴史博物館企画展示時に撮影。 ファイル:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|1号墳 盾持ち人

{{混同|塚廻古墳}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[ファイル:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|塚廻り4号墳 埴輪群（復元）}}]] '''塚廻り古墳群'''（つかまわりこふんぐん、塚廻古墳群）は、[[群馬県]][[太田市]]龍舞町にある[[古墳群]][[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]・熊野正也 編『日本古墳大辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1989年、373-374頁。。4号墳は[[群馬県指定文化財一覧#史跡|群馬県指定史跡]]に指定され、古墳群出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 現在では、4号墳は3号墳前方部とともに古墳公園として復元整備されている[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html　塚廻り古墳群第4号古墳　太田市]。 == 一覧 == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+塚廻り古墳群の一覧 |- ! 古墳名 ||規模 || 形状 || 備考 |- | 1号墳4号墳北北西約300mの位置に所在 || 26.1m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 削平 |- | 2号墳4号墳北西約300mの位置に所在|| 24m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 削平 |- | 3号墳4号墳北に所在|| {{Sdash}} || 帆立貝型古墳 || 一部保存 |- | 4号墳|| 22.5m || 帆立貝型古墳 || 復元、県史跡 |- | 5号墳4号墳南南西約90mの位置に所在|| 16m || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 6号墳4号墳北西約290mの位置に所在、2号墳のすぐ南|| 19m || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 7号墳4号墳北北西約200mの位置に所在|| 20m || 円墳 || 削平 |- | 8号墳 || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 9号墳 || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || 削平 |- | 10号墳4号墳南の近接地の地表下に所在|| {{Sdash}} || 帆立貝型古墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 11号墳4号墳南西の近接地の地表下に所在 || 16.1m || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 12号墳4号墳西の近接地の地表下に所在 || 9.5m || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |- | 13号墳4号墳北西の近接地の地表下に所在 || {{Sdash}} || 円墳 || 埋め戻し保存 |} == 画像 == 注記以外は群馬県立歴史博物館企画展示時に撮影。 ファイル:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|1号墳 盾持ち人

''Nihon K..."
revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. File:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Shield-Bearing Figure
{{small|Exhibited at the Gunma Archaeological Center.}} File:塚廻り1号古墳出土 埴輪 靫.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Quiver File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Male File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の女子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Female File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 盾.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Shield File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 跪座の男子.JPG|Kneeling Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|Seated Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる女子.JPG|Female Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 坏を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Cup File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 捧げ持つ男子.JPG|Male Holding an Offering File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-1.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-1.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-2.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-2.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-1.JPG|Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-2.JPG|Sword == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group (archaeological materials) - Details below. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Supplementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]]

{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ''' From No. 1 Kofun: '''* Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ''' From No. 3 Kofun: '''* Seated Female Figure (1) '''* Shields (2) ''' From No. 4 Kofun: '''* Female Figures (2) '''* Horse (1) '''* Swords (with stands, 2) ''' Supplementary Designations: '''* Human fragments (26) '''* Horse fragments (3) '''* Sword fragments (9) '''* Quiver fragments (2) '''* Shield fragments (5) '''* House fragment (1) '''* Base fragments (including whole bases) '''* Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōta, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun Clusters]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsukamawari Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsukamawari Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Tsukamawari Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Tsukamawari Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]]

{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ''' From No. 1 Kofun: '''* Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ''' From No. 3 Kofun: '''* Seated Female Figure (1) '''* Shields (2) ''' From No. 4 Kofun: '''* Female Figures (2) '''* Horse (1) '''* Swords (with stands, 2) ''' Supplementary Designations: '''* Human fragments (26) '''* Horse fragments (3) '''* Sword fragments (9) '''* Quiver fragments (2) '''* Shield fragments (5) '''* House fragment (1) '''* Base fragments (including whole bases) '''* Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. File:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Shield-Bearing Figure
{{small|Exhibited at the Gunma Archaeological Center.}} File:塚廻り1号古墳出土 埴輪 靫.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Quiver File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Male File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の女子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Female File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 盾.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Shield File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 跪座の男子.JPG|Kneeling Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|Seated Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる女子.JPG|Female Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 坏を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Cup File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 捧げ持つ男子.JPG|Male Holding an Offering File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-1.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-1.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-2.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-2.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-1.JPG|Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-2.JPG|Sword == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group (archaeological materials) - Details below. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Supplementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. File:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Shield-Bearing Figure
{{small|Exhibited at the Gunma Archaeological Center.}} File:塚廻り1号古墳出土 埴輪 靫.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Quiver File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Male File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の女子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Female File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 盾.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Shield File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 跪座の男子.JPG|Kneeling Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|Seated Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる女子.JPG|Female Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 坏を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Cup File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 捧げ持つ男子.JPG|Male Holding an Offering File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-1.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-1.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-2.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-2.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-1.JPG|Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-2.JPG|Sword == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group (archaeological materials) - Details below. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Supplementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1989, pp. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. File:塚廻り1号墳出土 埴輪 盾持ち人.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Shield-Bearing Figure
{{small|Exhibited at the Gunma Archaeological Center.}} File:塚廻り1号古墳出土 埴輪 靫.JPG|No. 1 Kofun: Quiver File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Male File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の女子.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Seated Female File:塚廻り3号古墳出土 埴輪 盾.JPG|No. 3 Kofun: Shield File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 跪座の男子.JPG|Kneeling Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 倚座の男子.JPG|Seated Male File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる女子.JPG|Female Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 坏を持つ女子.JPG|Female Holding a Cup File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 捧げ持つ男子.JPG|Male Holding an Offering File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-1.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-1.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 左手を挙げる男子-2.JPG|Male Raising Left Hand File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 飾り馬-2.JPG|Decorative Horse File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-1.JPG|Sword File:塚廻り4号古墳出土 埴輪 大刀-2.JPG|Sword == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group (archaeological materials) - Details below. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Supplementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]]

{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Suqqlementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] **Tsukamawari Kofun Group** (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{Confusion|塚廻古墳}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! revision.comment: Text replacement - "|塚廻古墳}}" to "|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}"
revision.text:
{{Confusion|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shuqqan, 1989, qq. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ !
