[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.285389|N|139.423861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q18337361]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamawari Kofun|qq=}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Su11lementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚廻り古墳群|version=96110307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337361}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q18337361}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Su11lementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚廻り古墳群|version=96110307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337361}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.285389|N|139.423861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18337361]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Not to be confused with {{ill|11|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamawari Kofun|qq=}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, [[:en:Ōta, Gunma|Ōta, Gunma]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Kofun Name || Size || Shape || Notes |- | No. 1 KofunLocated about 300 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 26.1 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 2 KofunLocated about 300 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 24 m || Scallop-shaped || Leveled |- | No. 3 KofunLocated north of No. 4 Kofun. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Partially preserved |- | No. 4 Kofun || 22.5 m || Scallop-shaped || Restored, Prefectural Historic Site |- | No. 5 KofunLocated about 90 m south-southwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 16 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 6 KofunLocated about 290 m northwest of No. 4 Kofun, just south of No. 2 Kofun. || 19 m || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 7 KofunLocated about 200 m north-northwest of No. 4 Kofun. || 20 m || Circular || Leveled |- | No. 8 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 9 Kofun || {{Sdash}} || {{Sdash}} || Leveled |- | No. 10 KofunLocated just south of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Scallop-shaped || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 11 KofunLocated just southwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 16.1 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 12 KofunLocated just west of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || 9.5 m || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |- | No. 13 KofunLocated just northwest of No. 4 Kofun, beneath the surface. || {{Sdash}} || Circular || Backfilled for preservation |} == Images == Unless otherwise noted, images were taken during exhibitions at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Su11lementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚廻り古墳群|version=96110307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337361}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Su11lementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚廻り古墳群|version=96110307|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Commons category|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337361}} [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)]][[ja:塚廻り古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Ota|WD=Q386179|1=Ōta, Gunma}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.285389|N|139.423861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Coord|36|17|07.4|N|139|25|25.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:塚廻り古墳 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|300px|{{center|Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun Haniwa Group (Restored)}}]] '''Tsukamawari Kofun Group''' (塚廻り古墳群, Tsukamawari Kofun-gun) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) located in Ryūmaicho, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|WD=Q386179|lt=Ōta, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan.Ōtsuka, Hajime; Kobayashi, Saburō; Kumano, Masaya, eds. ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia). Tokyo: Tōkyōdō Shu11an, 1989, 11. 373–374. The No. 4 Kofun is designated as a [[Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Site]], while artifacts excavated from the group are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Currently, the No. 4 Kofun and part of the No. 3 Kofun's front section have been restored and preserved as a kofun park.[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunmazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage. == Kofun List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ ! Owned by the national government (Agency for Cultural Affairs), preserved at the Gunma Prefectural Museum of History. Designated on June 6, 1985.{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} ** From No. 1 Kofun: *** Shield-Bearing Male Figure (1) ** From No. 3 Kofun: *** Seated Female Figure (1) *** Shields (2) ** From No. 4 Kofun: *** Female Figures (2) *** Horse (1) *** Swords (with stands, 2) ** Su11lementary Designations: *** Human fragments (26) *** Horse fragments (3) *** Sword fragments (9) *** Quiver fragments (2) *** Shield fragments (5) *** House fragment (1) *** Base fragments (including whole bases) *** Cylindrical haniwa (including fragments) === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun - Designated on September 20, 1977.{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture Homepage). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10022|Haniwa from Tsukamawari Kofun Group}} * [https://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/bunkazai/otabunka47.html Tsukamawari Kofun Group No. 4 Kofun] - Ōta City Homepage {{Redirect Category|redirect1=Tsukamawari No. 4 Kofun|1-1=Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚廻り古墳群|version=96110307|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)}} {{wikidata link|Q18337361|co|commons:Category:Tsukamawari Kofun Group (Ota)|ja|塚廻り古墳群}} [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
